newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Today's -5.0% move has no fundamental driver; it is flow-driven profit-taking off the June 2 pivot top (~16% below that high after an ~80% 90-day run). Backdrop items predate the window: June 3 UAV edge-AI MoU (Accton/Gallopwave/Hsuan Yuan), May 19 Google I/O Coralboard with Google Research, Q3 FY26 print (Core IoT +31% YoY). Sell-side chase continues post-rerating: Rosenblatt PT $180 (from $125), Wells Fargo $155 (from $120), June 8 roundup notes bullish targets building - a crowding signal, not new fundamentals.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - Synaptics edge-AI UAV collaboration MoU
GlobeNewswire - Google Research and Synaptics Coralboard at Google I/O 2026

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's removal call was structural - weak direct HBM/packaging/CPO bottleneck relevance - and nothing since June 6 changes that. Today's -5% is flow-driven and too small against +70.6% YTD to meaningfully restore asymmetry, while accelerating sell-side PT raises ($155-$180) signal growing crowding.
verdicts.bull: Core IoT grew 31% YoY and the Google Research Coralboard plus Astra SL2610/Torq NPU design-win cadence gives real edge-AI substance; a continued pullback would rebuild entry asymmetry.
verdicts.neutral: Edge-AI optionality is genuine but peripheral to this page's compute-bottleneck lens; a -5% flow day after an 80% 90-day run leaves the post-rerating setup essentially unchanged.
verdicts.bear: Consumer/IoT cyclicality plus a crowded post-rerating tape ($129 vs. $126 consensus target) means residual alpha is thin and momentum unwind can extend well beyond -5%.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete Astra/Torq edge-AI design wins converting to revenue guidance upside at the next print.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Momentum unwind in a crowded edge-AI story with weak direct exposure to the AI compute bottleneck.

verdicts.rankRationale: Low CPO exposure remains the binding constraint on this page and no CPO-relevant evidence emerged since June 6; yesterday's remove verdict stands and a flow-driven -5% day does not change the setup at rank 111.
verdicts.bull: If edge-AI silicon (Astra/Torq, Coralboard ecosystem) keeps compounding design wins, SYNA earns its optionality slot even without CPO relevance, and today's dip modestly improves entry.
verdicts.neutral: Nothing new ties SYNA to co-packaged optics; the stock is simply digesting a large rerating, which is orthogonal to this page's thesis.
verdicts.bear: Zero incremental CPO linkage plus IoT/consumer cycle risk and a +70% YTD chase makes this a dead slot on a CPO alpha list - the prior removal logic is intact.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any credible optical/connectivity attach for Astra-class edge silicon (none evident today).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Permanent irrelevance to the CPO supply chain combined with consumer/IoT cycle downturn.
